title: ForwardingBuffer: evicted packets not counted in statistics
description:
File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:211-219

When a source is evicted to make room for a new one, the evicted packets are not counted:

```python
if len(self._buffer) >= self.max_sources:
    # Evict oldest source (LRU)
    oldest_iid = self._source_order.pop(0)
    evicted_count = len(self._buffer.pop(oldest_iid))
    logger.debug("...evicting source %s (%d packets)", ...)
    result = ForwardingResult.EVICTED
    # NOTE: evicted_count is calculated but never added to any statistic!
```

The class tracks packets_accepted, packets_backpressure, and packets_expired, but NOT packets_evicted.

WHY IT'S A BUG:
1. Incomplete statistics - operators cannot see how many packets were lost to eviction
2. get_stats() returns an incomplete picture of buffer behavior
3. evicted_count is computed but discarded - wasted computation

IMPACT: Operators cannot diagnose buffer pressure issues; silent packet loss goes untracked.

FIX: Add packets_evicted counter:
```python
packets_evicted: int = 0
...
# In try_buffer:
self.packets_evicted += evicted_count
```
notes: Commit: 53a2720 (ForwardingBuffer packets_evicted stat implemented and tested in get_stats)
close_reason: Fixed: packets_evicted counter added to ForwardingBuffer, incremented on LRU eviction, exposed in get_stats(), and verified in test_get_stats. Related validation and other buffer fixes also implemented.
